title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Crosses $1 Billion Worldwide
text: James Cameron's latest installment has reached a significant milestone. The film is still No. 1 after three weeks of release and has achieved worldwide earnings of $1.083 billion, marking Cameron's fourth consecutive film to surpass the $1 billion threshold. After just 17 days in theaters, Avatar: Fire and Ash earned $306 million domestically, placing it 41st on the all-time charts. The film generated $40 million during its third weekend, outperforming comparable releases from earlier years. Industry analysts speculate the film is headed over $1.5 billion to become another all-timer for the director. With its strong performance trajectory and the $400+ million budget already recouped, the sequel appears positioned for sustained box office success in coming weeks. The 2025 domestic box office concluded with $8.65 billion in total revenue, the second-best year since the pandemic, and Avatar: Fire and Ash contributed significantly to that performance.
